volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 164 - Section 273)

sections.label: Section 164
sections.translation: Take your advice ? In what way ?

sections.label: Section 164b
sections.translation: In what way ? Well , firstly ,

sections.label: Section 165
sections.translation: do not fly in all directions with open beak ; it is not dignified . Among us , when we see a thoughtless man , we ask , What sort of bird is this ? and Teleas answers , It ' s a man who has no brain , a bird that has lost his head , a creature

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: ἀτέκμαρτος , οὐδὲν οὐδέποτʼ ἐν ταὐτῷ μένων .
sections.translation: you cannot catch , for it never remains in any one place .

sections.label: Section 171
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Διόνυσον εὖ γε μωμᾷ ταυταγί .
sections.translation: By Zeus himself ! your jest hits the mark . What then is to be done ?

sections.label: Section 172b
sections.translation: Found a city .

sections.label: Section 173
sections.original: ποίαν δʼ ἂν οἰκίσαιμεν ὄρνιθες πόλιν ;
sections.translation: We birds ? But what sort of city should we build ?

sections.label: Section 174
sections.translation: Oh , really , really ! you talk like such a fool !

sections.label: Section 175
sections.translation: Look down .

sections.label: Section 175b
sections.translation: I am looking .

sections.label: Section 175c
sections.translation: Now look up .

sections.label: Section 176
sections.translation: I am looking .

sections.label: Section 176b
sections.translation: Turn your head round .

sections.label: Section 176c
sections.translation: Ah ! it will be pleasant for me if I end in twisting my neck off !

sections.label: Section 178
sections.translation: What have you seen ?

sections.label: Section 178b
sections.original: τὰς νεφέλας γε καὶ τὸν οὐρανόν .
sections.translation: The clouds and the sky .

sections.label: Section 179
sections.original: οὐχ οὗτος οὖν δήπου ʼστὶν ὀρνίθων πόλος ;
sections.translation: Very well ! is not this the pole of the birds then ?

sections.label: Section 180
sections.translation: How their pole ?

sections.label: Section 180b
sections.translation: Or , if you like it , their place . And since it turns and passes through the whole universe , it is called ‘ pole .’ If you build and fortify it , you will turn your pole into a city .

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἄρξετʼ ἀνθρώπων μὲν ὥσπερ παρνόπων ,
sections.translation: In this way you will reign over mankind as you do over the grasshoppers and you will cause the gods to die of rabid hunger .

sections.label: Section 187
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 187b
sections.original: ἐν μέσῳ δήπουθεν ἀήρ ἐστι γῆς .
sections.translation: The air is between earth and heaven . When we want to go to Delphi , we ask the Boeotians for leave of passage ;

sections.label: Section 190
sections.translation: in the same way , when men sacrifice to the gods , unless the latter pay you tribute , you exercise the right of every nation towards strangers and don ' t allow the smoke of the sacrifices to pass through your city and territory .

sections.label: Section 194
sections.translation: By earth ! by snares ! by network ! by cages !

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: μὴ ʼγὼ νόημα κομψότερον ἤκουσά πω ·
sections.translation: I never heard of anything more cleverly conceived ; and , if the other birds approve , I am going to build the city along with you .

sections.label: Section 198
sections.original: τίς ἂν οὖν τὸ πρᾶγμʼ αὐτοῖς διηγήσαιτο ;
sections.translation: Who will explain the matter to them ?

sections.label: Section 198b
sections.translation: You must yourself . Before I came they were quite ignorant ,

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: ἐδίδαξα τὴν φωνήν , ξυνὼν πολὺν χρόνον .
sections.translation: but since I have lived with them I have taught them to speak .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.translation: But how can they be gathered together ?

sections.label: Section 201b
sections.translation: Easily . I will hasten down to the thicket to waken my dear Procne

sections.label: Section 205
sections.translation: and as soon as they hear our voices , they will come to us hot wing .

sections.label: Section 206
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ὀρνίθων σὺ μή νυν ἕσταθι ·
sections.translation: My dear bird , lose no time , please ! Fly at once into the thicket and awaken Procne .

sections.label: Section 209
sections.original: ἄγε σύννομέ μοι παῦσαι μὲν ὕπνου ,
sections.translation: Chase off drowsy sleep , dear companion .

sections.label: Section 210
sections.translation: Let the sacred hymn gush from thy divine throat in melodious strains ; roll forth in soft cadence your refreshing melodies to bewail the fate of Itys , which has been the cause of so many tears to us both .

sections.label: Section 215
sections.translation: Your pure notes rise through the thick leaves of the yew-tree right up to the throne of Zeus , where Phoebus listens to you , Phoebus with his golden hair . And his ivory lyre responds to your plaintive accents ;

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: he gathers the choir of the gods and from their immortal lips pours forth a sacred chant of blessed voices .

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ βασιλεῦ τοῦ φθέγματος τοὐρνιθίου ·
sections.translation: Oh ! by Zeus ! what a throat that little bird possesses . He has filled the whole thicket with honey-sweet melody !

sections.label: Section 225
sections.translation: Hush !

sections.label: Section 225b
sections.translation: What ' s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 225c
sections.translation: Be still !

sections.label: Section 225d
sections.translation: What for ?

sections.label: Section 226
sections.translation: Epops is going to sing again .

sections.label: Section 227
sections.translation: Epopopoi popoi popopopoi popoi , here , here , quick , quick , quick , my comrades in the air ;

sections.label: Section 230
sections.translation: all you who pillage the fertile lands of the husbandmen , the numberless tribes who gather and devour the barley seeds , the swift flying race that sings so sweetly . And you whose gentle twitter

sections.label: Section 235
sections.translation: resounds through the fields with the little cry of tiotiotiotiotiotiotiotio ; and you who hop about the branches of the ivy in the gardens ;

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: τά τε κατʼ ὄρεα τά τε κοτινοτράγα τά τε κομαροφάγα ,
sections.translation: the mountain birds , who feed on the wild olive-berries or the arbutus , hurry to come at my call , trioto , trioto , totobrix ; you also , who snap up the sharp-stinging

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἐμπίδας κάπτεθʼ , ὅσα τʼ εὐδρόσους γῆς τόπους
sections.translation: gnats in the marshy vales , and you who dwell in the fine plain of Marathon , all damp with dew , and you , the francolin with speckled wings ;

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: ὧν τʼ ἐπὶ πόντιον οἶδμα θαλάσσης
sections.translation: you too , the halcyons , who flit over the swelling waves of the sea , come hither to hear the tidings ; let all the tribes of long-necked birds assemble here ;

sections.label: Section 255
sections.translation: know that a clever old man has come to us , bringing an entirely new idea and proposing great reforms . Let all come to the debate here , here , here , here .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.translation: Torotorotorotorotix , kikkabau , kikkabau , torotorotorolililix .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.translation: Can you see any bird ?

sections.label: Section 263b
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Ἀπόλλω ʼγὼ μὲν οὔ ·
sections.translation: By Phoebus , no ! and yet I am straining my eyesight to scan the sky .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: ἄλλως ἄρʼ οὕποψ , ὡς ἔοικʼ , ἐς τὴν λόχμην
sections.translation: It was hardly worth Epops ' while to go and bury himself in the thicket like a hatching plover .

sections.label: Section 267
sections.translation: Torotix , torotix .

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: ὦγάθʼ ἀλλʼ εἶς οὑτοσὶ καὶ δή τις ὄρνις ἔρχεται .
sections.translation: Wait , friend , there ' s a bird .

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ ὄρνις δῆτα . τίς ποτʼ ἐστίν ; οὐ δήπου ταὧς ;
sections.translation: By Zeus , it is a bird , but what kind ? Isn ' t it a peacock ?

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: οὗτος αὐτὸς νῷν φράσει · τίς ἐστιν ὄρνις οὑτοσί ;
sections.translation: Epops will tell us . What is this bird ?

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: οὗτος οὐ τῶν ἠθάδων τῶνδʼ ὧν ὁρᾶθʼ ὑμεῖς ἀεί ,
sections.translation: It ' s not one of those you are used to seeing ; it ' s a bird from the marshes .

sections.label: Section 272b
sections.translation: Oh ! oh ! but he is very handsome with his wings as crimson as flame .

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: εἰκότως γε · καὶ γὰρ ὄνομʼ αὐτῷ ʼστὶ φοινικόπτερος .
sections.translation: Undoubtedly ; indeed he is called flamingo .
